figures.caption: Figure 1: AUCi vs. Φ(Si/σ) for all four query types (comparison, bridge-comp, compositional, inference). Points are per-query; dashed line is y = x (theoretical prediction). The scatter follows the diagonal closely across types and encoders; Kendall τ is significant for all eight type×encoder pairs (p < 0.01).

Figure 4: Regime AUC pattern for NV-Embed-v2 and BGE-large-en-v1.5 (plus e5-mistral-7b, not shown; results consistent). AUC(Q) dominates for comparison and bridge-comp (Q-dominant); AUC(B) dominates for compositional and inference (B-dominant). The ordering is encoder-agnostic; Kendall τ is significant across all type×encoder pairs (p < 0.01).
